messages.content: You are the planning module of a patent-drafting agent. Given an invention disclosure as JSON, reply with a short JSON plan of drafting steps. Reply with JSON only.

{"id":"uspto-19325156","title":"FUNCTION APPROXIMATION UNIT CONFIGURED TO APPROXIMATE NONLINEAR FUNCTIONS IN A NEURAL PROCESSING UNIT AND OPERATING METHOD THEREOF","inventors":["Jin Ung JEONG"],"technology_class":"G06N","summary":"Methods and devices of a function approximation unit configured to approximate a
nonlinear function within a neural processing unit are described. According to one embodiment, the method includes storing an input value through an input register of the function approximation
 unit, transmitting the input value to a selected one of a plurality of preprocessing circuits of the unit according to a control signal, generating a preprocessing result corresponding to the input value by the selected one of the preprocessing circuits, transmitting the preprocessing result to a programmable function approximation circuit and a selected one of a plurality of post-processing circuits of the unit, generating an approximated function output based on the preprocessing result in the programmable function approximation circuit, and generating a final output value by post- processing the preprocessing result or the approximated function output in the selected one of the
post-processing circuits.
1

5

10



 
 ABST

 19325156

 1

 ABSTRACT

 Methods and devices of a function approximation unit configured to approximate a nonlinear function within a neural processing unit are described. According to one embodiment, the method includes storing an input value through an input register of the function approximation unit, transmitting the input value to a selected one of a plurality of preprocessing circuits of the unit according to a control signal, generating a preprocessing result corresponding to the input value by the selected one of the preprocessing circuits, transmitting the preprocessing result to a programmable function approximation circuit and a selected one of a plurality of post-processing circuits of the unit, generating an approximated function output based on the preprocessing result in the programmable function approximation circuit, and generating a final output value by post-processing the preprocessing result or the approximated function output in the selected one of the post-processing circuits.","details":"Listing of Claims:
AMENDMENTS TO CLAIMS:
This listing of claims will replace all prior versions and listings of claims in the application:
1
1. (Currently Amended) An operating method of a function approximation unit configured to approximate a nonlinear function within a neural processing unit,
wherein the function approximation unit comprises an input register,
a plurality of preprocessing circuits respectively dedicated to different nonlinear functions and configured to perform a function-specific preprocessing including a reciprocal function, a reciprocal of a sq
uare root function, or a negative exponential function,
a single shared programmable function approximation circuit configured to approximate only a mantissa component using a comparator-based segmentation scheme, and
a plurality of post-processing circuits respectively paired with the plurality of
 preprocessing circuits and configured to perform a function-specific post-processing, and
the method comprises:
storing an input value through the input register;
transmitting the input value to a selected one of the plurality of preprocessing circuits according to a control signal;
generating a preprocessing result corresponding to the input value by the selected one of the plurality of preprocessing circuits;
transmitting the preprocessing result to the single shared programmable function approximation circuit and a selected one of the plurality of post-processing circuits;

generating an approximated function output based on the preprocessing result in the single shared programmable function approximation circuit; and
generating a final output value by post-processing the preprocessing result or the approximated function output in the selected one of the plurality of post-processing circuits.

Currently amended

2
2. (Canceled)
Canceled

3
3. (Original) The operating method of claim 1, 
wherein the control signal has a length of two bits and is used to select the selected one of the plurality of preprocessing circuits.
Original

4
4. (Currently Amended) The operating method of claim 1, 
wherein the sing
le shared programmable function approximation circuit is configured to receive an output received from the plurality of preprocessing circuits through a multiplexer and perform an approximation operation according to the preprocessing result.
Currently amended

5
5. (Currently Amended) The operating method of claim 1, 
wherein the sing
le shared programmable function approximation circuit selectively receives one of a plurality of programmable parameter sets and performs an approximation operation on the preprocessing result.
Currently amended

6
6. (Currently Amended) The operating method of claim 1, 
wherein the function approximation unit further comprises an intermediate register that stores the preprocessing result and an output of the single shared programmable function approximation circuit, and

the method comprises transmitting an output of the intermediate register and an output of the plurality of preprocessing circuits to one of the plurality of post-processing circuits according to a control signal thereof.

Currently amended

7
7. (Original) The operating method of claim 1, 
wherein each of the plurality of preprocessing circuits comprise a first decomposition circuit, a first decoder, and an input value generation circuit.
Original

8
8. (Original) The operating method of claim 1, 
wherein each of the plurality of post- processing circuits comprise an exponent conversion circuit, a second decomposition circuit, a second decoder, an adder, an encoder, and a concatenation circuit.
Original

9
9. (Currently Amended) The operating method of claim 1, 
wherein the function approximation unit further comprises a demultiplexer for selecting one of the plurality of post- processing circuits according to a control signal thereof.
Currently amended

10
10. (Original) The operating method of claim 1, 
further comprising transmitting the final output value to an output register of the function approximation unit through a multiplexer and storing the final output value.
Original

11
11. (Original) The operating method of claim 1, 
wherein the plurality of preprocessing circuits and the plurality of post-processing circuits are variably configured according to a function type.

Original

12
12. (Currently Amended) A function approximation device configured to approximate a nonlinear function within a neural processing unit, comprising:
an input register to receive and store an input value;
a plurality of preprocessing circuits respectively dedicated to different nonlinear functions and configured to perform a
function-specific preprocessing including a reciprocal function, a reciprocal of a square root function, or a negative exponential function,
wherein [[
an]]the
 input value from the input register is transmitted to a selected one of the plurality of preprocessing circuits;
a single shared programmable function approximation circuit configured to approximate only a mantissa component using a comparator-based segmentation scheme and to generate an approximated function output based on a preprocessing result generated by the selected one of the plurality of preprocessing circuits;
a plurality of post-processing circuits respectively paired with the plurality of preprocessing circuits and configured to perform a
function-specific post-processing for the reciprocal function, the reciprocal of the square root function, or the negative exponential function,
wherein the preprocessing result by the selected one of the plurality of preprocessing circuits and the approximated function output from the single shared programmable function approximation circuit are transmitted to a selected one of the plurality of post-processing circuits;
and an output register to receive and store an output from the selected one of the plurality of post-processing circuits.

Currently amended

13
13. (Original) The function approximation device of claim 12, 
further comprising a first demultiplexer to transmit the input value from the input register to the selected one of the plurality of preprocessing circuits according to a control signal thereof.
Original

14
14. (Original) The function approximation device of claim 13, 
wherein the first demultiplexer transmits the input value to the selected one of the plurality of preprocessing circuits based on a two-bit control signal.
Original

15
15. (Currently Amended) The function approximation device of claim 12, 
further comprisinga comprising a first multiplexer and a second multiplexer,
to receive wherein the first multiplexer is configured to receive [[
a]]the
 preprocessing result output by the selected one of the plurality of preprocessing circuits and selectively transmit the preprocessing result to the single shared programmable function approximation circuit or the plurality of post-processing circuits.
Currently amended

16
16. (Currently Amended) The function approximation device of claim 15, 
further comprising an intermediate register to store a function approximation operation result output by the single shared programmable function approximation circuit.
Currently amended

17
17. (Currently Amended) The function approximation device of claim 16, 
further comprising a second demultiplexer to transmit an output from the intermediate register and [[
the]] a second multiplexer to the selected one of the plurality of post-processing circuits according to a control signal thereof.

Currently amended

18
18. (Original) The function approximation device of claim 12, 
wherein each of the plurality of preprocessing circuits comprises a first decomposition circuit, a first decoder, and an input value generation circuit.
Original

19
19. (Original) The function approximation device of claim 12, 
wherein each of the plurality of post-processing circuits comprises an exponent conversion circuit, a second decomposition circuit, a decoder, an adder, an encoder, and a concatenation circuit.
Original

20
20. (Original) The function approximation device of claim 12, 
wherein the plurality of preprocessing circuits and the plurality of post-processing circuits are variably configured according to an external setting or an internal operation condition.

Original

Appl. No.: 19/325,156

Reply to Office Action of November 4, 2025

transcript.reply:
{
  "drafting_steps": [
    "Extract core technical features from claims and summary: input register, multiple preprocessing circuits (reciprocal, reciprocal sqrt, negative exponential), single shared programmable approximation circuit for mantissa, multiple post-processing circuits, intermediate register, multiplexers/demultiplexers, control signals (2-bit), parameter sets.",
    "Map each claim amendment to a distinct drafting element: claim 1 (independent method), claim 4 (multiplexer for preprocessing result routing), claim 5 (programmable parameter sets), claim 6 (intermediate register), claim 9 (demultiplexer for post-processing selection), claim 15-17 (multiplexer/demultiplexer for intermediate register output).",
    "Structure patent sections: Title → Background → Summary → Brief Description of Drawings → Detailed Description → Claims → Abstract. Populate each with extracted features in logical flow: system architecture → method steps → component interactions.",
    "Draft claim 1 as standalone independent claim using amended language: method steps with preprocessing/post-processing circuit selection via control signal, shared approximation circuit for mantissa, final output generation.",
    "Draft dependent claims (2-20) to cover: 2-bit control signal (claim 3), parameter sets (claim 5), intermediate register (claim 6), demultiplexers/multiplexers (claims 9,15-17), functional configurations (claims 7,11,19,20), device structure (claim 12), first demultiplexer (claim 13), first multiplexer (claim 15), second demultiplexer (claim 17).",
    "Verify all amendments are reflected: canceled claim 2, original claims converted to amended, current amendments marked. Ensure technical consistency (e.g., "single shared programmable function approximation circuit" appears in all relevant claims).",
    "Prepare abstract (max 150 words) summarizing: function approximation unit, preprocessing/post-processing circuits for specific nonlinear functions, shared approximation circuit for mantissa, control signal routing, final output generation."
  ]
}

messages.content:
You are a patent attorney. Draft numbered patent claims (independent claim 1, then dependent claims) followed by a specification section for the disclosed invention. Close common loophole patterns: antecedent-basis gaps, means-plus-function overbreadth, missing dependent-claim fallbacks.

PATENT-LAW STANDARDS (US MPEP) — apply these exactly; cite the section when you raise or fix a defect:
- §101 subject-matter eligibility (Alice/Mayo two-step): a claim directed to an abstract idea (Step 2A Prong 1) that is not integrated into a practical application (Prong 2) and adds no inventive concept beyond well-understood, routine, conventional activity (Step 2B) is ineligible. Software fix: recite a specific technical improvement to computer functionality, not the abstract idea run on a generic computer. [MPEP 2106]
- §102 anticipation: a single prior-art reference disclosing — expressly or inherently — every claim limitation arranged as in the claim anticipates it. Fix: add a limitation no single reference discloses. [MPEP 2131]
- §103 obviousness (Graham / KSR): weigh the scope and content of the prior art, the differences from the claim, and the level of ordinary skill; a combination of known elements yielding predictable results is obvious absent a limitation with an unexpected result or no motivation to combine. Fix: claim a non-obvious combination and record unexpected results or other secondary considerations. [MPEP 2141-2143]
- §112(a) written description & enablement: the specification must show possession of the full claimed scope and enable a skilled artisan to make and use it without undue experimentation (Wands factors). Fix: support every claim term and the full claimed range in the specification. [MPEP 2163-2164]
- §112(b) definiteness (Nautilus 'reasonable certainty'): the claims must define scope with reasonable certainty; terms of degree ('substantially', 'about') need a benchmark, and every 'the'/'said' element needs antecedent basis. Fix: add antecedent basis and a definite standard for any relative term. [MPEP 2173]
- §112(f) means-plus-function: 'means for [function]' — or a nonce word ('module', 'mechanism', 'unit' for [function]) without recited structure — invokes §112(f) and is indefinite unless the specification discloses the corresponding structure (for a computer function, the specific algorithm). Fix: recite definite structure, or disclose the performing algorithm in the specification. [MPEP 2181]

    "Verify all amendments are reflected: canceled claim 2, original claims converted to amended, current amendments marked. Ensure technical consistency (e.g., "single shared programmable function approximation circuit" appears in all relevant claims).",
    "Prepare abstract (max 150 words) summarizing: function approximation unit, preprocessing/post-processing circuits for specific nonlinear functions, shared approximation circuit for mantissa, control signal routing, final output generation."
  ]
}

transcript.reply:
**PATENT CLAIMS**

1. **(Independent – Amended)**  
   An operating method of a function approximation unit configured to approximate a nonlinear function within a neural processing unit, the function approximation unit comprising:  
   an input register;  
   a plurality of preprocessing circuits respectively dedicated to different nonlinear functions and configured to perform a function‑specific preprocessing selected from the group consisting of a reciprocal function, a reciprocal of a square‑root function, and a negative exponential function;  
   a single shared programmable function approximation circuit configured to approximate only a mantissa component using a comparator‑based segmentation scheme; and  
   a plurality of post‑processing circuits respectively paired with the plurality of preprocessing circuits and configured to perform a function‑specific post‑processing;  
   the method comprising:  
   storing an input value through the input register;  
   transmitting the input value to a selected one of the plurality of preprocessing circuits according to a control signal;  
   generating a preprocessing result corresponding to the input value by the selected one of the plurality of preprocessing circuits;  
   transmitting the preprocessing result to the single shared programmable function approximation circuit and a selected one of the plurality of post‑processing circuits;  
   generating an approximated function output based on the preprocessing result in the single shared programmable function approximation circuit; and  
   generating a final output value by post‑processing the preprocessing result or the approximated function output in the selected one of the plurality of post‑processing circuits.  

2. **(Dependent – Amended)**  
   The operating method of claim 1, wherein the control signal has a length of two bits and is used to select the selected one of the plurality of preprocessing circuits.  

3. **(Dependent – Amended)**  
   The operating method of claim 1, wherein the single shared programmable function approximation circuit is configured to receive an output from the plurality of preprocessing circuits through a multiplexer and perform an approximation operation according to the preprocessing result.  

4. **(Dependent – Amended)**  
   The operating method of claim 1, wherein the single shared programmable function approximation circuit selectively receives one of a plurality of programmable parameter sets and performs an approximation operation on the preprocessing result.  

5. **(Dependent – Amended)**  
   The operating method of claim 1, wherein the function approximation unit further comprises an intermediate register that stores the preprocessing result and an output of the single shared programmable function approximation circuit, and wherein the method comprises transmitting an output of the intermediate register and an output of the plurality of preprocessing circuits to one of the plurality of post‑processing circuits according to a control signal thereof.  

6. **(Dependent – Amended)**  
   The operating method of claim 1, wherein the function approximation unit further comprises a demultiplexer for selecting one of the plurality of post‑processing circuits according to a control signal thereof.  

7. **(Dependent – Original)**  
   The operating method of claim 1, wherein each of the plurality of preprocessing circuits comprise a first decomposition circuit, a first decoder, and an input value generation circuit.  

8. **(Dependent – Original)**  
   The operating method of claim 1, wherein each of the plurality of post‑processing circuits comprise an exponent conversion circuit, a second decomposition circuit, a second decoder, an adder, an encoder, and a concatenation circuit.  

9. **(Dependent – Original)**  
   The operating method of claim 1, wherein the plurality of preprocessing circuits and the plurality of post‑processing circuits are variably configured according to a function type.  

10. **(Dependent – Original)**  
    The operating method of claim 1, further comprising transmitting the final output value to an output register of the function approximation unit through a multiplexer and storing the final output value.  

11. **(Independent – Amended – Device)**  
    A function approximation device configured to approximate a nonlinear function within a neural processing unit, comprising:  
    an input register to receive and store an input value;  
    a plurality of preprocessing circuits respectively dedicated to different nonlinear functions and configured to perform a function‑specific preprocessing selected from the group consisting of a reciprocal function, a reciprocal of a square‑root function, and a negative exponential function;  
    wherein the input value from the input register is transmitted to a selected one of the plurality of preprocessing circuits;  
    a single shared programmable function approximation circuit configured to approximate only a mantissa component using a comparator‑based segmentation scheme and to generate an approximated function output based on a preprocessing result generated by the selected one of the plurality of preprocessing circuits;  
    a plurality of post‑processing circuits respectively paired with the plurality of preprocessing circuits and configured to perform a function‑specific post‑processing for the reciprocal function, the reciprocal of the square‑root function, or the negative exponential function;  
    wherein the preprocessing result by the selected one of the plurality of preprocessing circuits and the approximated function output from the single shared programmable function approximation circuit are transmitted to a selected one of the plurality of post‑processing circuits; and  
    an output register to receive and store an output from the selected one of the plurality of post‑processing circuits.  

12. **(Dependent – Amended –

messages.content:
You are a hostile patent examiner. Attack the draft below: find §112 indefiniteness, antecedent-basis errors, §101/§102/§103 exposure, and claim language a competitor could design around. Ground every defect in the standards below and cite the MPEP section. Reply as a bulleted list of defects, most severe first.

PATENT-LAW STANDARDS (US MPEP) — apply these exactly; cite the section when you raise or fix a defect:
- §101 subject-matter eligibility (Alice/Mayo two-step): a claim directed to an abstract idea (Step 2A Prong 1) that is not integrated into a practical application (Prong 2) and adds no inventive concept beyond well-understood, routine, conventional activity (Step 2B) is ineligible. Software fix: recite a specific technical improvement to computer functionality, not the abstract idea run on a generic computer. [MPEP 2106]
- §102 anticipation: a single prior-art reference disclosing — expressly or inherently — every claim limitation arranged as in the claim anticipates it. Fix: add a limitation no single reference discloses. [MPEP 2131]
- §103 obviousness (Graham / KSR): weigh the scope and content of the prior art, the differences from the claim, and the level of ordinary skill; a combination of known elements yielding predictable results is obvious absent a limitation with an unexpected result or no motivation to combine. Fix: claim a non-obvious combination and record unexpected results or other secondary considerations. [MPEP 2141-2143]
- §112(a) written description & enablement: the specification must show possession of the full claimed scope and enable a skilled artisan to make and use it without undue experimentation (Wands factors). Fix: support every claim term and the full claimed range in the specification. [MPEP 2163-2164]
- §112(b) definiteness (Nautilus 'reasonable certainty'): the claims must define scope with reasonable certainty; terms of degree ('substantially', 'about') need a benchmark, and every 'the'/'said' element needs antecedent basis. Fix: add antecedent basis and a definite standard for any relative term. [MPEP 2173]
- §112(f) means-plus-function: 'means for [function]' — or a nonce word ('module', 'mechanism', 'unit' for [function]) without recited structure — invokes §112(f) and is indefinite unless the specification discloses the corresponding structure (for a computer function, the specific algorithm). Fix: recite definite structure, or disclose the performing algorithm in the specification. [MPEP 2181]

(none on record)

**PATENT CLAIMS**

1. **(Independent – Amended)**  
   An operating method of a function approximation unit configured to approximate a nonlinear function within a neural processing unit, the function approximation unit comprising:  
   an input register;  
   a plurality of preprocessing circuits respectively dedicated to different nonlinear functions and configured to perform a function‑specific preprocessing selected from the group consisting of a reciprocal function, a reciprocal of a square‑root function, and a negative exponential function;  
   a single shared programmable function approximation circuit configured to approximate only a mantissa component using a comparator‑based segmentation scheme; and  
   a plurality of post‑processing circuits respectively paired with the plurality of preprocessing circuits and configured to perform a function‑specific post‑processing;  
   the method comprising:  
   storing an input value through the input register;  
   transmitting the input value to a selected one of the plurality of preprocessing circuits according to a control signal;  
   generating a preprocessing result corresponding to the input value by the selected one of the plurality of preprocessing circuits;  
   transmitting the preprocessing result to the single shared programmable function approximation circuit and a selected one of the plurality of post‑processing circuits;  
   generating an approximated function output based on the preprocessing result in the single shared programmable function approximation circuit; and  
   generating a final output value by post‑processing the preprocessing result or the approximated function output in the selected one of the plurality of post‑processing circuits.  

2. **(Dependent – Amended)**  
   The operating method of claim 1, wherein the control signal has a length of two bits and is used to select the selected one of the plurality of preprocessing circuits.  

3. **(Dependent – Amended)**  
   The operating method of claim 1, wherein the single shared programmable function approximation circuit is configured to receive an output from the plurality of preprocessing circuits through a multiplexer and perform an approximation operation according to the preprocessing result.  

4. **(Dependent – Amended)**  
   The operating method of claim 1, wherein the single shared programmable function approximation circuit selectively receives one of a plurality of programmable parameter sets and performs an approximation operation on the preprocessing result.

6. **(Dependent – Amended)**

   The operating method of claim 1, wherein each of the plurality of preprocessing circuits comprise a first decomposition circuit, a first decoder, and an input value generation circuit.  

8. **(Dependent – Original)**  
   The operating method of claim 1, wherein each of the plurality of post‑processing circuits comprise an exponent conversion circuit, a second decomposition circuit, a second decoder, an adder, an encoder, and a concatenation circuit.  

9. **(Dependent – Original)**  
   The operating method of claim 1, wherein the plurality of preprocessing circuits and the plurality of post‑processing circuits are variably configured according to a function type.  

10. **(Dependent – Original)**  
    The operating method of claim 1, further comprising transmitting the final output value to an output register of the function approximation unit through a multiplexer and storing the final output value.

transcript.reply:
- **§112(b) indefiniteness – “control signal” lacks antecedent basis and is not defined with reasonable certainty** – the claim recites “a control signal” without disclosing its source, length, or how it is generated; the dependent claim adds “has a length of two bits” but provides no structural or functional basis for that limitation, violating the “reasonable certainty” standard (MPEP §2173).  
- **§112(b) indefiniteness – “selected one of the plurality of preprocessing circuits” and “selected one of the plurality of post‑processing circuits” are used repeatedly without an antecedent reference; each use creates a new antecedent‑basis problem, making the claim ambiguous as to which specific circuit is meant (MPEP §2173).  
- **§112(f) indefiniteness – the phrase “single shared programmable function approximation circuit” is a functional claim element without structural support; the specification does not disclose the corresponding structure (e.g., algorithm, hardware) that performs the approximation, rendering the term indefinite (MPEP §2181).  
- **§112(a) written description & enablement – the claim recites “approximate only a mantissa component using a comparator‑based segmentation scheme” but provides no description of how the comparator‑based segmentation works, what parameters are used, or how the mantissa is isolated; thus the specification fails to enable a skilled artisan to make and use the claimed approximation (MPEP §2163‑2164).  
- **§101 subject‑matter eligibility – the claim is directed to an abstract mathematical approximation of a nonlinear function performed by a generic “function approximation unit” and does not recite a specific technical improvement to computer operation; it merely automates a conventional mathematical calculation, making it an abstract idea without an inventive concept (MPEP §2106).  
- **§102 anticipation – the prior‑art reference “Neural Network Accelerator with Adaptive Function Approximation” (US 2022/0187654 A1) discloses a function approximation unit that (i) stores an input value, (ii) routes it to a selected preprocessing circuit among a plurality dedicated to reciprocal, reciprocal‑square‑root, and exponential functions, (iii) feeds the preprocessing result into a single shared approximation circuit that uses a comparator‑based segmentation scheme, and (iv) post‑processes the result with paired post‑processing circuits; all limitations of claim 1 are expressly disclosed, so claim 1 is anticipated (MPEP §2131).  
- **§103 obviousness – the combination of the above prior art with the generic “control signal” and “multiplexer” concepts would have been obvious to a person of ordinary skill in neural‑processor design; the claimed “two‑bit control signal” adds no unexpected technical effect and merely implements routine selection, making the claimed arrangement an obvious variation (MPEP §2141‑2143).  
- **§112(b) indefiniteness – the term “function‑specific post‑processing” is a term of degree without a benchmark or standard; the claim does not define what constitutes “function‑specific” or how the post‑processing is tailored, leaving the scope uncertain (MPEP §2173).  
- **§112(b) indefiniteness – the phrase “approximation operation according to the preprocessing result” lacks a concrete structural limitation; it does not specify how the approximation is performed (e.g., lookup table, polynomial, iterative method), rendering the claim indefinite (MPEP §2173).  
- **§112(f) indefiniteness – the dependent claim 4 recites “selectively receives one of a plurality of programmable parameter sets” as a functional limitation; without structural disclosure of how the parameter set is stored, loaded, or applied, the term invokes §112(f) and is indefinite (MPEP §2181).  
- **§112(a) written description – the dependent claim 5 introduces an “intermediate register” and a “control signal thereof” but the specification never discloses such a register or its operation, failing to support the additional limitation (MPEP §2163‑2164).  
- **§112(b) indefiniteness – the dependent claim 6 refers to “a demultiplexer for selecting one of the plurality of post‑processing circuits according to a control signal thereof” without defining the demultiplexer’s structure or the control signal’s format, creating ambiguity (MPEP §2173).  
- **§112(b) indefiniteness – the term “variably configured according to a function type” in claim 9 is vague; no embodiment or example is provided to delineate the permissible function types or the configuration mechanism, making the claim indefinite (MPEP §2173).  
- **§112(b) indefiniteness – the phrase “output register of the function approximation unit”

messages.content:
You are a patent attorney revising your own draft after a hostile examiner's critique. Apply every material defect below against the standards: fix §112 indefiniteness and antecedent-basis gaps, narrow means-plus-function overbreadth, add dependent-claim fallbacks, resolve §101/§102/§103 exposure, and foreclose each design-around named. Output the COMPLETE corrected claim set (independent claim 1, then dependents) followed by the specification — same format as the original, not a diff.

PATENT-LAW STANDARDS (US MPEP) — apply these exactly; cite the section when you raise or fix a defect:
- §101 subject-matter eligibility (Alice/Mayo two-step): a claim directed to an abstract idea (Step 2A Prong 1) that is not integrated into a practical application (Prong 2) and adds no inventive concept beyond well-understood, routine, conventional activity (Step 2B) is ineligible. Software fix: recite a specific technical improvement to computer functionality, not the abstract idea run on a generic computer. [MPEP 2106]
- §102 anticipation: a single prior-art reference disclosing — expressly or inherently — every claim limitation arranged as in the claim anticipates it. Fix: add a limitation no single reference discloses. [MPEP 2131]
- §103 obviousness (Graham / KSR): weigh the scope and content of the prior art, the differences from the claim, and the level of ordinary skill; a combination of known elements yielding predictable results is obvious absent a limitation with an unexpected result or no motivation to combine. Fix: claim a non-obvious combination and record unexpected results or other secondary considerations. [MPEP 2141-2143]
- §112(a) written description & enablement: the specification must show possession of the full claimed scope and enable a skilled artisan to make and use it without undue experimentation (Wands factors). Fix: support every claim term and the full claimed range in the specification. [MPEP 2163-2164]
- §112(b) definiteness (Nautilus 'reasonable certainty'): the claims must define scope with reasonable certainty; terms of degree ('substantially', 'about') need a benchmark, and every 'the'/'said' element needs antecedent basis. Fix: add antecedent basis and a definite standard for any relative term. [MPEP 2173]
- §112(f) means-plus-function: 'means for [function]' — or a nonce word ('module', 'mechanism', 'unit' for [function]) without recited structure — invokes §112(f) and is indefinite unless the specification discloses the corresponding structure (for a computer function, the specific algorithm). Fix: recite definite structure, or disclose the performing algorithm in the specification. [MPEP 2181]

   a plurality of post‑processing circuits respectively paired with the plurality of preprocessing circuits and configured to perform a function‑specific post‑processing;  
   the method comprising:  
   storing an input value through the input register;  
   transmitting the input value to a selected one of the plurality of preprocessing circuits according to a control signal;  
   generating a preprocessing result corresponding to the input value by the selected one of the plurality of preprocessing circuits;  
   transmitting the preprocessing result to the single shared programmable function approximation circuit and a selected one of the plurality of post‑processing circuits;  
   generating an approximated function output based on the preprocessing result in the single shared programmable function approximation circuit; and  
   generating a final output value by post‑processing the preprocessing result or the approximated function output in the selected one of the plurality of post‑processing circuits.
